mechanism: Inhibits multiple tyrosine kinases including FLT3, blocking oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Broad TKI with reported activity in FLT3‑driven AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL family kinases and other kinases, impairing proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Broad TKI; can show cytotoxicity or be useful in combinations in AML

mechanism: Inhibits BCL2 family proteins (BCL2, BCL‑XL), inducing apoptosis by releasing pro‑apoptotic factors
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; potent apoptosis inducer in some AML models

mechanism: Inhibits 26S proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Often active in low‑nM to sub‑µM range; note suggestion to consider lower top conc (e.g., 100 nM) if highly sensitive

mechanism: Irreversible inhibitor of the proteasome catalytic activity, inducing apoptosis from proteotoxic stress
additional_notes: Second‑generation proteasome inhibitor with potency in hematologic malignancies

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases broadly, altering chromatin and gene expression to reduce proliferation/induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Pan‑HDAC; expected low‑to‑mid nM to sub‑µM IC50 in sensitive AML lines

mechanism: Allosteric MEK1/2 inhibition, blocking MAPK pathway signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: MAPK pathway inhibitor; may be useful in combinations where MAPK is active

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and proliferation via PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway suppression
additional_notes: mTOR pathway commonly active in AML; antiproliferative effects expected

mechanism: Selective inhibition of PI3Kδ isoform, disrupting survival signaling downstream of PI3K/AKT
additional_notes: More active in lymphoid malignancies but reported activity in some AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs including CDK9, suppressing transcription of short‑half‑life survival proteins (e.g., MCL1) and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as flavopiridol; tested in AML clinical studies targeting transcriptional addiction

mechanism: Inhibits HSP90, promoting degradation of client oncoproteins (e.g., FLT3) and inducing cell death
additional_notes: Also called tanespimycin; degrades multiple oncogenic kinases including FLT3, cytotoxic in AML lines
